title: fuzzer: harden JSON input boundary against non-object scalars 🌪️
summary: Added a guard at the FFI JSON entrypoint (`run_json_inner`) to explicitly check if the parsed JSON is an object. Previously, valid JSON scalars like `"123"` or `null` were blindly parsed into `serde_json::Value` causing downstream property queries to either return `None` (falling back to defaults silently) or potentially panic.
proof_summary: Added explicit fuzz/object validation tests (`crates/tokmd-core/tests/ffi_object_verification_w73.rs`) verifying that `123` and `[]` are properly rejected with `invalid_settings` before progressing.
rollback: Remove `if !args.is_object() { return Err(TokmdError::invalid_field("args", "a JSON object")); }` from `crates/tokmd-core/src/ffi.rs`.
